LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BIOMET INC., BIOMET ORTHOPEDICS INC., BIOMET ORTHOPEDICS LLC. AND BIOMET BIOLOGICS, LLC'S MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY CLAIMS FOR FORUM NON CONVENIENS AND TO SEVER CLAIMS. CONTINUED TO JUNE 15, 2012 PER THE AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, DISCUSSED AND THE PARTIES SUBMITTED TO THE TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS BIOMET INC., BIOMET ORTHOPEDICS INC., BIOMET BIOLOGICS, LLC AND BIOMET BIOLOGIES, LLC.'S AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY CLAIMS FOR FORUM NON CONVENIENS AND TO SERVER CLAIMS IS GRANTED. THE CLAIMS OF THE NON-CALIFORNIA PLAINTIFFS ARE SEVERED FROM THE CALIFORNIA PLAINTIFF AND THE CLAIMS OF THE NON-CALIFORNIA PLAINTIFFS ARE STAYED PENDING A DETERMINATION THAT ALL DEFENDANTS ARE SUBJECT TO JURISDICTION IN THE HOME STATES OR OTHER FORUMS CHOSEN BY THE NON-CALIFORNIA PLAINTIFFS TO PURSUE THEIR CLAIMS. CALIFORNIA IS A SERIOUSLY INCONVENIENT FORUM TO ADJUDICATE THE CLAIMS OF THE NON-CALIFORNIA PLAINTIFFS AND THE PUBLIC AND PRIVATE FACTORS WEIGH IN FAVOR OF HAVING THE CLAIMS OF THOSE PLAINTIFFS DECIDED IN THEIR HOME STATES RATHER THAN CALIFORNIA. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-10-2012 Off Calendar

Amended Ntc Of Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Claims
Off Calendar Jun-15-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joshua B. Marker, Esq., from Reed Smith LLP, 415-659-5693, for Defendant and moving party
Lowell W. Finson, Esq., from Phillips Webster, LIPS, 213-808-6746, for Plaintiff and opposing party, appearing by telephone

Discussed and the parties submitted to the tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS BIOMET INC., BIOMET ORTHOPEDICS INC., BIOMET BIOLOGICS, LLC AND BIOMET BIOLOGIES, LLC.'S Amended Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Claims for Forum non conveniens and to server claims is Granted. The claims of the non-California plaintiffs are severed from the California plaintiff and the claims of the non-California plaintiffs are stayed pending a determination that all defendants are subject to jurisdiction in the home states or other forums chosen by the non-California plaintiffs to pursue their claims. California is a seriously inconvenient forum to adjudicate the claims of the non-California plaintiffs and the public and private factors weigh in favor of having the claims of those plaintiffs decided in their home states rather than California. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077

Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Claims For Forum Non Conveniens And To Sever Claims
Off Calendar Jun-14-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS BIOMET INC., BIOMET ORTHOPEDICS INC., BIOMET ORTHOPEDICS LLC. AND BIOMET BIOLOGICS, LLC'S Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Claims For Forum Non Conveniens And To Sever Claims. Continued to June 15, 2012 per the amended notice of motion. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077
